title: ajuga chamaepitys subsp. tridactylites
intro: Ajuga chamaepitys subsp. tridactylites, commonly known as bugle, is a perennial plant species native to Europe and parts of Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing stress and anxiety, improving sleep quality, and supporting immune function. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of Ajuga chamaepitys subsp. tridactylites are typically dried and used as a tea or infused in oil for topical applications.
